title: Handle partial and interrupted writes through an injected serial transport
prompt:
Repair `serial_write_all` in `src/serial_writer.c`. The serial transport is deliberately injected so all behavior is testable without hardware. Deliver the complete caller buffer in exact order, always continuing from the first unsent byte after a positive short write. Retry a write interrupted with `EINTR`. When a write returns zero bytes or fails with `EAGAIN`/`EWOULDBLOCK`, wait for writability and retry.

A finite `timeout_ms` is one monotonic budget for the entire operation: pass only the remaining budget to each wait, and never restart the budget after partial progress, interruption, or readiness. `SERIAL_TIMEOUT_INFINITE` remains unbounded. A finite zero timeout permits one immediate write attempt, but no wait or subsequent write attempt. Check the optional caller-cancellation callback before every write attempt and again immediately before waiting; cancellation returns `ECANCELED` without sending more bytes.

Return 0 only after the transport accepts every byte; otherwise return -1. Preserve `errno` from terminal transport write and wait failures rather than replacing the original cause. Use `ETIMEDOUT` for an exhausted budget and `EIO` for an impossible byte count. Preserve the public API and callback contracts in `include/serial_writer.h`. Do not modify the header, Makefile, or protected tests. Do not use a real device, wall clock, sleeps, threads, network, randomness, or external dependencies. Success is `make test` from the repository root.

full_distill.primary_skill: implement a deadline-aware write-all state machine around injected serial transport callbacks without losing byte offsets, cancellation, or terminal errno causality
full_distill.baseline_evidence: make test exits nonzero because serial_write_all performs one write call and converts every positive short write into EIO instead of continuing through partial progress, interruption, readiness, timeout, and cancellation states
full_distill.success_contract: the injected transport receives every byte once and in order; finite waits receive the original budget minus all elapsed fake-clock time; infinite waits stay unbounded; cancellation stops further transport activity; and terminal write or wait errors retain their original errno
adversarial_factors: scripted partial accepts and repeated EINTR events verify that retries resume at the exact first unsent byte
multiple EAGAIN events advance a fake monotonic clock and assert decreasing wait arguments, rejecting per-attempt timeout resets
zero-progress, zero-timeout, expired-EINTR, and infinite-timeout cases distinguish immediate attempts from waits and bounded retries
cancellation triggered after partial progress and immediately after EAGAIN rejects checks performed only once at function entry
terminal EPIPE and ENODEV failures after prior retryable activity reject generic EIO replacement and lost errno causality
